extra.message: User input from request.form.get('username') and request.form.get('password') is concatenated directly into SQL query string without parameterization. This allows SQL injection attacks to bypass authentication or extract data.
User input from request.cookies.get('session') is directly embedded into HTML response without escaping. This allows stored XSS attacks through cookie manipulation.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from request.args.get('q') is directly embedded into HTML response without escaping. This allows reflected XSS attacks via the query parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The /dashboard endpoint has no authentication checks. Any user can access it by directly navigating to the URL, bypassing the login mechanism. The endpoint only relies on an insecure session cookie.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials (USERNAME and PASSWORD) are stored directly in source code. These credentials can be easily discovered by inspecting the application code.
Session is stored in a plain-text cookie with the username value without encryption, signing, or secure flags. An attacker can forge or manipulate the session cookie.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: The dashboard endpoint reflects arbitrary JavaScript code embedded in the string. Combined with the ability to set session cookies via SQL injection, this creates a stored XSS vulnerability.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Flask application is configured to run on 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) and bound to port 80 without explicit debug=False setting. This may expose sensitive information in error pages.
